Submit to Laboratory
Choose a monitoring type below and submit it as a separate lab-verified report
Each monitoring type is submitted as its own separate report. Air Quality, Water Quality, Noise, Soil etc. are tracked and verified independently by the NABL laboratory. Types shown in green have saved data ready to submit.

Recently Submitted

Track Submission Status
Follow each report through the full Lab → Regulatory → Certificate workflow
FLOW: ① Submitted ② Lab Review ③ Lab Decision ④ Regulatory ⑤ Certificate ● Approved = Green ● Rejected = Red

EIA Compliance Score
Walk through all 9 EIA sections step-by-step. Instantly see your compliance score across all environmental parameters & download your certificate.
Check My Score
What's Included — EIA Report
  • ✦Company Profile & Branding — logo, address, team members, contact info
  • ✦Project Information — name, type, industry, location, land area
  • ✦Environmental Baseline — air quality, water, noise, soil, ecology
  • ✦Impact Assessment Matrix — magnitude, significance, mitigation
  • ✦Daily Monitoring Logs — auto-compiled into report appendix
  • ✦Professional PDF — MoEFCC EIA 2006 · ISO 14001 · CPCB format

  • ✦Noise, Waste & Biodiversity — CPCB norms, PCHWM compliance
  • ✦Instant Compliance Score — overall % with pillar-wise breakdown
  • ✦Download Certificate — EIA compliance rating certificate PDF

AI Carbon Emission Analysis
Carbon Footprint — Current vs Previous Report
Current Previous — Min safe — Max limit

Carbon Emission Limits & Regulatory Specifications

CategoryParameterBenchmark / LimitStandard / AuthorityConsequence if Exceeded
Scope 1Total Direct EmissionsAs per PAT Scheme targetBEE / MoEF&CCPAT Cycle penalty up to ₹10 lakh/day
Scope 1Stack Emission (CO₂)<150 mg/Nm³ (for thermal plants)CPCB / MoEF Notification 2015Closure order / ETP upgrade required
Scope 2Grid Electricity Emission Factor0.82 kg CO₂e/kWh (India national grid)CEA (2023–24)Disclosure under SEBI BRSR required
Scope 3Supply Chain GHG IntensityDisclose top 3 suppliers' emissionsCDP / GHG ProtocolESG rating downgrade risk
All ScopesNet Zero Target (India)Net Zero by 2070 (NDC pledge)UNFCCC Paris AgreementRegulatory / investor pressure
All ScopesCarbon Intensity Reduction45% reduction in GDP intensity by 2030India NDC (2023 Update)Non-compliance with national targets
ReportingSEBI BRSR DisclosureTop 1000 listed companies: mandatorySEBI BRSR (2022–23)Stock exchange listing penalty
ReportingCarbon Credit (PAT Scheme)1 ESCert = 1 tonne oil equivalent savedBEE Perform Achieve TradePenalty: 1.5× market price of shortfall
Carbon Emission Reporting & Reduction Instructions
📏 How to Measure Scope 1 Measure fuel quantities (litres/m³ of diesel, LPG, natural gas). Use IPCC emission factors (e.g. Diesel: 2.68 kg CO₂e/litre). Stack emissions require CEMS (Continuous Emission Monitoring). Record monthly and maintain logbook.
⚡ How to Calculate Scope 2 From electricity bills: kWh × 0.82 = tCO₂e (CEA factor). For renewable energy, factor = 0. Use location-based method for BRSR reporting. Separate DG set usage from grid consumption.
🌐 Estimating Scope 3 Business travel: use DEFRA/ICAO emission factors. Commuting: employee survey + distance × mode factor. Supply chain: request Scope 1+2 data from top 3 suppliers. Waste: tonnes × treatment method factor.
🎯 Reduction Strategies 1. Switch to renewable energy (solar/wind) to cut Scope 2. 2. Electrify vehicle fleet → reduces Scope 1. 3. Improve boiler/furnace efficiency → BEE star rating. 4. Plant trees / carbon credits for offset. 5. Set SBTi-aligned target (1.5°C pathway).
GHG Assessment
Greenhouse gas inventory, compliance scoring & reduction roadmap —
GHG Protocol Corporate Standard ISO 14064-1 BRSR Core SBTi 1.5°C Pathway
Mandatory GHG Disclosure — Your Industry
This industry has mandatory greenhouse gas disclosure requirements under national and international frameworks. Use the tools below to complete your assessment.
GHG Emission Inventory — Scope 1, 2 & 3
GHG Scope Inventory
Quantify Scope 1 (direct), Scope 2 (energy) and Scope 3 (value chain) emissions. Compute total GHG footprint with AI-powered analysis.
Open Inventory
GHG Compliance Assessment
Score your GHG management practices against GHG Protocol, ISO 14064 & BRSR Core. Identify gaps and receive actionable recommendations.
Start Assessment
Reduction Target Planner
Set SBTi-aligned emission reduction targets for 2030 & 2050. Define abatement strategies and track decarbonisation progress.
Set Targets
Industry-Specific GHG Reporting Requirements
Industry Primary GHG Sources Regulatory Framework Disclosure
Manufacturing Fuel combustion, industrial processes, fugitive emissions GHG Protocol, BEE PAT Scheme, BRSR Core Mandatory
Chemical Process reactions, refrigerant releases, boiler combustion GHG Protocol, CPCB Emission Norms, BRSR Core Mandatory
Power Generation Coal / gas combustion, SF₆ from T&D, auxiliary fuel use CEA Stack Norms, RPO, BRSR Core, SBTi Mandatory
Mining Diesel machinery, coal-bed methane, blasting emissions GHG Protocol, EIA Notification 2006, MMDR Act Recommended
Oil & Gas HC fugitive leaks, flaring & venting, combustion PPAC MRV Framework, GHG Protocol OGP, BRSR Core Mandatory
Aviation Jet fuel combustion, APU emissions, ground support ICAO CORSIA, UNFCCC, BRSR Core, GHG Protocol Mandatory
Automotive Paint shop VOCs, test facility fuel, supply chain Scope 3 CAFE Norms (BEE), GHG Protocol, BRSR Core Mandatory
Construction On-site diesel, embedded concrete CO₂, demolition waste GHG Protocol, IGBC GreenPro, EIA 2006, ISO 50001 Recommended
Cement & Concrete Calcination process CO₂, kiln fuel, clinker production CSI GHG Protocol, BEE PAT Scheme, BRSR Core Mandatory
Paper & Pulp Black liquor combustion, fossil fuel boilers, wastewater CH₄ GHG Protocol, BEE PAT Scheme, BRSR Core Mandatory
GHG Reduction Target Planner
Set emission reduction targets aligned with the Paris Agreement 1.5°C pathway (SBTi methodology)
